title: IDACC Wiki Manifest
description: Compact app-readable navigation and source manifest. Human wiki content is being rebuilt as Markdown under docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.md.

editContract.format: Minimal JSON manifest for the desktop app. Keep prose short; put long-form docs in Markdown.

pages.purpose: Operator home: view the current routed team lead, talk to that lead, watch cross-team coordination, and read the live activity feed.
pages.scope: All-team observation with a read-only chat target. The embedded chat dispatches to the routed team lead; the activity feed merges events across teams.
pages.body:
# Dashboard

Dashboard is the operator home for live fleet status, routed lead chat, and recent activity. Keep this page lightweight; detailed behavioral history belongs in release notes, not the live wiki manifest. The renderer store coalesces and version-stamps sync domains so page remounts observe the latest invalidation state without extra broad refreshes. Electron builds use the main-process sync stream as the single mutation invalidation source, while non-Electron transports retain local fallback invalidation. Broad all-team roster polling is kept on a moderate cadence while the event stream keeps activity fresh. Dashboard and Work keep the faster all-team roster cadence for active coordination, while heavier secondary pages keep their initial cross-team context but poll more slowly and hidden windows back off further. Live Coordination mirrors HR Manager's full org roster from hierarchy teams, configured coordinators, secondary coverage, and all-agent team tags; stopped or idle teams remain visible instead of disappearing from the tree. The embedded lead chat preserves manager error/text details while confirming failed or expired queries, so operator triage sees the actionable failure rather than a bare status. The shared status bar derives active agent and team counts from the already-loaded all-team roster when available, falling back to the team-lead IPC poll only on views without that roster.

pages.purpose: Operator decision queue: answer blockers surfaced by agents, Work Plans, Work Learn review gates, and manager inbox items waiting on a human reply, with store-level duplicate suppression.
pages.scope: All local blocker questions across teams plus manager inbox from the live store snapshot.
pages.body:
# Inbox

Inbox is the review surface for blocker questions and approval prompts. It should stay focused on actionable pending decisions and their source context. Work Learn review prompts use normalized material sources, including host-like URLs without an explicit scheme, before kind and title detection. Learn blocker review now remains separate from Brain graph sync state: source-trust questions stay actionable here, while material-to-Brain sync evidence is recorded on the Learn material. Work plan actions can now surface deferred lead routing separately from hard failures, so review copy should preserve that distinction. Work plan audit/blocker preflight transport failures stay local to the Plans row while delegation continues, so rate limits and query timeouts do not create human decisions. Work plan blockers render as recovery cards with the recommended action, plan context, and collapsed technical detail instead of leading with raw agent failure text. Work plan recovery decisions resolve locally through the main-process `plans:recover` pass: approve/retry/review-style answers resume the Brain plan and retry team-lead delegation, while hold/pause/manual answers keep the plan paused. Synthetic plan answers are not sent back as chat text to the lead.

pages.purpose: Work operating surface: goals, plans, Learn material ingestion, live Kanban, recurring schedules, agent chains, dreams, and hidden context-budgeted dispatch.
pages.scope: All-team task view for the board; creation and assignment panels target execution candidates by default, with explicit team-lead fanout for lead-driven delegation.
notes: Autopilot master now defaults on with a 15-minute cadence and bounded fanout; active goal triggers immediately queue Brain instruction sync and team-lead work checks.
pages.body:
# Work

Work owns goals, plans, tasks, Learn intake, schedules, loops, and dreams. Task creation and assignment should be explicit, manager-routed, and guarded against draft/replay duplication. Background draft dispatch is disabled by default; Dashboard draft rows remain review-only unless an operator explicitly opts into `draftDispatcher.enabled`. Create Work's normal assignment scopes show execution targets, not coordinators, and plan proposal owner dropdowns use the same execution-candidate set so primary/team leads do not accidentally become default task owners; team-lead fanout remains the explicit path for lead-driven delegation. Goal-driver caps count open goal-tagged tasks across every routed team for default/global goals, so a cross-team fanout cannot respawn just because the default team's own board is empty. Active Autopilot goals first try the ops task-manager/task-master planner, then fall back to lead planning, and finally create bounded deterministic team-lead tasks when planners are blocked so a busy planner cannot stall goal progress entirely. Work > Plans keeps its delegated execution objectives tagged as plan-origin records, and normal Goals/Teams/Learn/goal-driver reads hide those internal plan objectives unless a caller explicitly asks for plan tracking records. Learn materials are auto-processed one at a time by the main process: queued rows drain in the background, material write/remove progress now publishes Learn sync changes while the tab is open, blocked rows remain only while blocking recommendations are still draft, and ready rows stay visible while their Brain graph sync is missing, stale, or short on required edges. Learn compares extracted material against active Goals only, gives stable recommendation IDs, and automatically creates guarded queued Work tasks from non-blocking task/feature recommendations that match active goals; source-trust blockers still pause automation until reviewed, then resume the same guarded task creation path. The Learn queue runner also backfills already-processed eligible recommendations with a cooldown on deferred/failed task attempts, so materials digested before automation was installed can still create queued Work tasks without duplicating rows. Completed Learn materials now require a current Brain graph sync ledger before they are hidden, and sync-pending rows expose a bounded Brain repair action instead of disappearing from the active queue. Completed Learn materials record a Brain sync result and write a connected graph packet: learn-material entity, source entity, routed team entities, matched goal entities, facts, entity edges, text unit, shared memory, and a `learn:material-synced` timeline event. Learn Brain sync schema v3 records exact required edge coverage as `edgeCount/expectedEdgeCount`, retries stale/partial graph packets, and reuses existing text chunks during backfill so graph repair does not duplicate ingested material. Ready/blocked material writes now kick the Brain backfill runner promptly, and manual Learn sync can force an immediate retry without waiting for the normal retry window. The Learn queue runner is event-kicked for new or terminal material writes, then parks idle polling at five minutes; Brain backfill remains prompt for ready material but parks idle checks at ten minutes so Learn/Brain state stays current without a 30s idle wake loop. Generic material save telemetry keeps queue state visible but only publishes shared digest memory after a ready/blocked material has summary, comparison, or Brain sync output. Material progress notifications stay scoped to the materials domain, with only terminal processing events refreshing Brain/Inbox; a separate `materials:tasks` sync event refreshes Tasks/Work/Dashboard only when Learn actually creates or defers Work tasks. Learn intake normalizes host-like source strings without a scheme, such as github.com/org/repo or eth.sh, to HTTPS before kind, title, and queue storage decisions; local paths, explicit PDF/folder imports, whitespace text, and already-schemed sources keep their input form. The Learn queue and review panes are constrained below intake so selected review content wraps inside its panel instead of crowding submission controls. Work fanout and decomposition defer instead of enqueueing more lead prompts when the target lead already has too many active queries. Advisory Work decomposition now prefers a live ops-team task-manager/task-master planner before falling back to the team lead, so primary leads are not consumed by JSON planning when a task manager is available. Work plan creation now snapshots owner and unassigned-queue capacity before calling `/task create`; items that would stack on an owner or todo backlog are reported as capacity-deferred without creating live manager rows. Work > Plans delegation now has a deterministic team-lead fallback: if planner delegation fails, IDACC still creates bounded coordination tasks for active non-default team leads, records unresolved delegation as Inbox triage, and invalidates task/work/dashboard/brain domains via `work:delegateToTeamLeads` so new rows render immediately. Work > Plans Inbox recovery now runs through `plans:recover`, which saves/updates the objective, retries team-lead delegation, marks the Brain plan partial on success, or re-raises a more specific recovery blocker if delegation still cannot be created. Work > Plans action buttons track in-flight state per plan file, allowing separate rows and Work next to be triggered concurrently while guarding each individual plan from duplicate work/status writes. If Inbox question writes fail, the Plans row records `decision write failed` instead of silently swallowing the blocker. When the manager accepts a task but returns a queue/backlog warning, Work leaves it queued and does not send a separate /ask that would bypass the manager guard. Lead selection uses explicit lead metadata and coordinator/router roles before generic manager names. Shared sync-domain rules must keep read calls non-invalidating so Work-adjacent pages do not reload themselves in loops.

pages.purpose: Local project tracker with workspace discovery, metadata editing, GitHub import, git status, and git operation requests.
pages.scope: App-local settings store plus filesystem/git/GitHub helpers in the main process.
pages.body:
# Projects

Projects tracks local workspace repositories and reviewed project actions. It should describe current operator flows, not release history.

pages.purpose: HR Manager Health tab for fleet health, local-model token throughput, liveness probes, and the full fleet roster with harness/model/lifecycle controls, neutral provider model-lane visibility, and read-only lead markers. Hidden context-budget savings are backend-only and not displayed here.
pages.scope: Embedded in HR Manager between Structure and Build; the legacy health route opens this tab. Usage spans local-model providers and refreshes as its own observability stream; roster is holistic and grouped by team through AgentTable. Harness/model lane reads stay unsubscribed until an operator opens model lanes or focuses a runtime/model picker, then use runtime-catalog scoped caches across tab remounts so ordinary agent roster churn, settings edits, module refreshes, and page switches do not reload provider state.
pages.body:
# HR Manager Health

Health is the fleet observation and lifecycle surface inside HR Manager. It shows running state, runtime evidence, and guarded probe/lifecycle controls. Harness and model selectors use cached passive state by default; focusing a selector keeps runtime details warm for five minutes, while the model-lanes panel pins runtime catalog details and cooldown polling. Passive runtime model, freshness, and subscription reads are held in the shared renderer cache for the same five-minute window as subscription status, and selector focus, Settings sync chatter, and tab switches reuse that snapshot instead of reloading the harness catalog. HR Manager still receives all-team roster context for the graph and routing views, but it now uses the slower secondary all-team poll cadence after the initial load; explicit re-check, lifecycle actions, and page-level force loads remain the live refresh paths for provider, CLI model, and roster evidence.

pages.purpose: Manage agent onchain identity, wallet controller address, Safe-style account, and scoped session keys.
pages.scope: All-team agent roster with wallet-bearing agents sorted first. Key provider is currently mock unless a live provider is wired behind the same interface.
pages.body:
# Identity & Keys

Identity & Keys owns controller proof, wallet provisioning, scoped key actions, and the guarded contract execution console for agent.bittrees.eth. Keep this page focused on current authority and safety boundaries. The contract console is wallet/Safe-mediated only: it validates the connected Safe address, supported chain, target address, calldata, and wei value, requires a current eth_call simulation preview plus human confirmation, then hands submit to wallet/Safe approval. It does not read private keys, change Safe signers, grant permissions, or execute through an IDACC backend signer. Shared API query lifecycle types may include active-query depth for routing guards; identity reads that only inspect settings should not broadcast page invalidations, create tasks, or dispatch work. BrainClient remains the shared typed Brain channel for controller reads and graph writes; entity-edge helpers now report accepted and expected edge counts for Work/Capabilities graph sync without changing Identity controller authority. Main-process background Learn/Brain runners stay outside identity authority: they publish material or Brain sync changes only and do not create wallet/controller mutations.

pages.purpose: Organization design, team building, roster lifecycle, selected-agent goals/instruction sidecars, hierarchy sync, secondary validation coverage, guarded team maintenance, and outbound relay topology.
pages.scope: All teams for graph/overview; active team for relay policy, hierarchy, and team operations; selected-agent instruction editing stays team-qualified from Structure.
pages.body:
# HR Manager

HR Manager owns team structure, hierarchy, routing, relays, selected-agent instructions, and team lifecycle controls. It is the source for delegation topology. The Build tab caches runtime/provider/subscription data separately from library skills: runtime selectors refresh only on runtime-catalog mutations, while skills refresh on modules mutations. Normal roster churn, unrelated Settings writes, and module-only refreshes do not stall the harness dropdowns. Structure, Route, and Health stay off the Build catalog subscription, passive Build catalog reads are coalesced in the shared runtime cache, and the renderer keeps a five-minute runtime catalog snapshot across short sync-version churn so the harness dropdown does not refetch on every tab switch or Settings echo. Selected-agent goal panels subscribe to goals/org/agents only, so Learn queue progress and unrelated Work churn do not reload HR goal state. The structure graph also compares the derived team/agent payload before setting React state, avoiding full graph churn when the all-teams roster poll reports the same fleet.

pages.purpose: Capability workbench for MCP tool servers, skills, and bundled plugins.
pages.scope: Active team, all teams, or all leads depending on scope selector. Runtime support is advisory; capability assignment remains runtime-neutral while stale target rows are still blocked by fresh-read guards.
pages.body:
# Capabilities

Capabilities owns skills, MCP servers, plugins, runtime-neutral capability wiring, and guarded Brain sync handoffs. Provider and runtime catalog reads stay cached/non-invalidating; provider connects, subscription account mutations, local model install/remove actions, and explicit runtime probes still refresh dependent views without passive CLI command execution. MCP attachment starts with no selected targets, because every attached server can spawn helper processes per active harness; skills and plugins still default to the current scope. The reference/test `everything` MCP server is parked and filtered from the catalog, local settings, and outgoing attach payloads. Material/Learn progress sync is scoped away from modules so Capabilities does not reload during queue processing, while the shared BrainClient can upsert entity edges and return accepted/expected counts for surfaces that need connected graph records. The shared background policy keeps Learn queue and Brain backfill work event-kicked and idle-backed-off, so capability selectors are not churned by background material processing. Dashboard draft/proposal reads are review-only; they do not auto-promote agent output into Tasks or dispatch assignments. The shared sync-domain rule for `work:delegateToTeamLeads` is scoped to Work/Tasks/Dashboard/Brain and should not wake Capabilities.

pages.purpose: Supervised local computer-control broker: bless capable agents, arm/disarm control, watch the screen, approve actions, and panic-stop.
pages.scope: Local machine. Requires macOS Screen Recording and Accessibility permissions; actions only run through the armed broker.
pages.body:
# Computer Use

Computer Use owns the local broker, blessed-agent attachment, approval flow, and immediate stop controls for agents that can drive the Mac. The Computer Use broker shares the main-process IPC surface, but Learn/Brain background policy changes must not wake or reload broker permission, approval, or stop-control state.

pages.purpose: Infrastructure panel for host hardware, manager connection, coordinator status with focused HR Manage handoff, self-update, subscriptions, local models, image server, local stacks, and inference backends.
pages.scope: Local app settings plus manager-facing provider/concurrency routes.
pages.body:
# Settings

Settings owns local manager connection, update status, provider/back-end configuration, local model setup, subscription sign-ins, and runtime availability evidence. Provider and subscription mutations now prime the shared runtime catalog cache with the fresh returned rows before any background freshness check, so HR runtime selectors can reuse Settings state instead of re-reading it. Passive account-status reads stay cached and non-invalidating without warming model catalogs on open/focus/timer; account sign-in/sign-out/install, provider sync, explicit runtime probe, and local model pull/remove/catalog actions publish runtime-catalog changes so HR runtime selectors refresh only when those inputs actually change. Other settings writes stay on the settings domain and do not invalidate HR harness dropdown caches, and ordinary runtime/status reads reuse the shared cache for up to 5 minutes. Draft dispatcher settings are opt-in: an absent `draftDispatcher.enabled` value does not start the background poller or promote draft proposals. Local stack install/background probes are coalesced while in flight, and the periodic local-runtime refresh relies on discovery's bundled background check instead of issuing a second duplicate status call.

pages.purpose: Live documentation and editable UI schematic for the Control Center itself.
pages.scope: Reads this JSON file through app-local IPC. Does not touch manager state.
pages.body:
# Wiki

This page now reads a compact app manifest. The replacement authoring contract is being rebuilt in docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.md as Markdown-first schema documentation. The renderer loads and polls the wiki manifest only while the Wiki page is open, keeping the rest of the app free from idle wiki refresh work.
